title: St. Vincent de Paul yogurt run — Sept 2020
html:
TL;DR

  • Tiny Apple Note from Sept 15, 2020 (Yahir age 17, senior year of HS) titled "Salvation". Three-line body: "At Vincent de Paul / Okay Store Store Riverside / Yogurt" — followed by the load-bearing line: "Maybe for once I am doing things right, giving me the confidence to continue."
  • Sets the scene at St. Vincent de Paul (Catholic charity thrift store in Grants Pass, on Riverside) — Yahir running a small errand during the covid lockdown senior year, just one yogurt in the basket. The "Salvation" title isn't theological — it's the felt-sense of doing one boring competent thing right.
  • This is one of the corpus's earliest vulnerability specimens — the bravado register that dominates 2019–2022 cracks here for one sentence. Sits in the same emotional bucket as the later Peacock incident and the 2025 "scraping by" inventory: rare moments where Yahir lets the inner monologue out.
  • The line "maybe for once I am doing things right" implies a baseline of feeling like things are going wrong — an inverse-mood specimen that contradicts the "obsessed with goals" / fear of not doing templates dominating the same era. Both registers are real; the vault should hold both.
  • "Okay Store Store Riverside" is a transcription error for Okay Re-Store (or a similar Riverside-Drive thrift). The double-word betrays voice-to-text input — common in 2020 Yahir notes. Companion specimens: 2020 08 10 ranch grocery vocab list, 2020 09 26 dream alexandria haircut card attack.

Key quote

"Maybe for once I am doing things right, giving me the confidence to continue."

Why it matters in the graph

  • Establishes st vincent de paul grants pass as a recurring grants pass teen era location — same orbit as micah 6 food pantry and hope food pantry. Together these draw a map of the charity-economy circuit a teen Yahir actually moved through.
  • Counter-evidence to the build-mode-bravado dominant register — useful when brain ask questions probe Yahir's actual emotional baseline.

Privacy

  • No PII in the source.

Wikilinks
